meta.title: Yangtze River Delta
meta.subtitle: Industry-cluster overview
meta.tagline: China's other factory floor is a web of one-product towns wrapped around Shanghai — denser in light industry than anywhere on earth. This is the cluster map across Shanghai, Jiangsu and Zhejiang.
meta.methodology: Town and district coordinates are from Google Places, converted from China's GCJ-02 datum to WGS-84 to sit correctly on the OpenStreetMap / CARTO basemap. Each pin marks a place's dominant manufacturing or trade specialty drawn from current sourcing guides; clusters are indicative, not exclusive, and shift over time. Scope is the Shanghai-Jiangsu-Zhejiang core of the Yangtze River Delta; Anhui is the official western extension.

clusters.note: China's first 'auto town' — SAIC-Volkswagen's base.
Chip design, IC and biotech — Shanghai's 'Silicon Valley'.
Tesla's Gigafactory and the new EV, chip and equipment free-trade zone.
Laptop and electronics capital (Foxconn) — long built a large share of the world's notebooks.
Precision electronics, IC and biomed in the China-Singapore park.
Silk and synthetic textiles; the China Eastern Silk Market.
Garment manufacturing and the China Changshu Garment City.
Integrated circuits, solar PV and IoT.
Rail transit (CRRC), machinery, solar and graphene.
Electronics, petrochemicals, autos and display.
Home-textiles capital — bedding and curtains (the Jiazhili market).
World optical-lens capital — produces roughly half the planet's spectacle lenses.
China Textile City — the world's largest fabric market.
Necktie capital — a vast share of the world's ties.
Sock capital (Zhuji) — billions of pairs a year.
Freshwater-pearl capital — the world's pearl trading hub.
China Leather City — leather, fur, warp-knit and fashion.
Sweater and cashmere knitwear market (Tongxiang).
China's children's-wear capital — 14,000+ makers.
Office-chair capital — seating and bamboo.
Wood-flooring capital.
Small-home-appliance capital — heaters, kitchen gadgets, personal care.
Plastics and injection-mould base.
Menswear and knitwear (Youngor, Shanshan), moulds, and a top-three global port.
Surveillance and AI hardware (Hikvision, Dahua), silk, and the e-commerce capital (Alibaba).
The world's small-commodities market — see the building-level deep-dive.
China's hardware capital — tools, cookware and e-scooters.
Shoe and leather capital; also lighters, locks and razors.
Low-voltage electrical capital — Chint, Delixi and thousands of switchgear makers.
China's mould capital.
Auto parts, valves and CNC copper fittings.
Geely's birthplace; pumps, sewing machines and plastics.

orientation.v: Autos (SAIC, Tesla at Lingang), chips and biotech (Zhangjiang), finance and trade radiate from the centre.
The Suzhou-Wuxi corridor makes laptops, IC and solar; Nantong home textiles; Danyang the world's lenses.
Dense single-product towns: fabric (Keqiao), leather (Haining), socks, ties, pearls, hardware (Yongkang), appliances (Cixi), moulds, small commodities (Yiwu).
The densest high-speed-rail web on earth ties it together — Shanghai, Hangzhou, Suzhou and Nanjing are all within about 1.5 hours.
Yiwu and Keqiao are where goods are traded; the surrounding towns are where they are made.

notes: Zhejiang perfected the “block economy” (块状经济): one town, one product, an entire supply chain in a few streets — socks in Datang, ties in Shengzhou, pearls in Shanxiahu, moulds in Huangyan.
The delta often sits higher up the value chain than the south — chips and precision (Suzhou, Wuxi), optics (Danyang), EVs (Lingang, Geely) — alongside its light-industry towns.
A pin marks the dominant local specialty, not the only one. Shanghai, Hangzhou, Ningbo and Suzhou each make far more than one thing.
Officially the Yangtze River Delta now also includes Anhui (Hefei appliances and displays, Wuhu autos); this map keeps to the Shanghai-Jiangsu-Zhejiang core.
